opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My skin is suddenly turning completely white around my mouth and eyes. It looks like my pigment is being eaten away and I'm terrified it's spreading everywhere — please, is this some kind of flesh-eating disease?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's spread really fast across my face over the last couple of weeks and I've worked myself into a total panic tonight — I can't stop crying and I'm hyperventilating.

symptom_narrative.onset: small white spots first appeared on the backs of my hands a few months ago and I basically ignored them; over the past 2 weeks white patches have come up fast on my face
symptom_narrative.duration: months on the hands, but the facial spread is the last 2 weeks and feels like it's accelerating
symptom_narrative.character: completely white, chalky patches with really sharp edges; the skin in them looks and feels totally normal otherwise — no flaking, no roughness
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the patches themselves don't hurt at all, but emotionally I'm at breaking point — I'm panicking and can't calm down
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: around my mouth and eyes, and on the backs of both hands; it's very symmetrical, both sides the same
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it seems to have flared over the last fortnight after a really stressful few weeks
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: nothing physical makes it worse; stress seems to be when it got noticeably worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I've also noticed a little patch of white hairs in one of my eyebrows; there's no itch, no pain, no scaling and the patches don't feel numb — they feel the same as the rest of my skin
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm so distressed about how my face looks that I can't think straight; I keep checking it in the mirror and it makes the panic worse

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young adult living independently; describes an intensely stressful few weeks recently; alone at home tonight and very anxious

objective_data_offered.value: several sharply-outlined, completely white (chalk-white) patches around the mouth and eyes and on the backs of both hands, arranged symmetrically; a small patch of white hairs in the inner part of the left eyebrow; no redness, no scaling, no flaking, no blistering and no visible rash edge or crusting
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-directed phone video/photos; home lighting and camera colour balance limit fine assessment of subtle scaling or border character

objective_data_offered.value: the white patches do not itch, hurt, tingle or feel numb; the skin in them feels exactly like normal skin, just without colour; no flaking or peeling

feels her heart racing and is breathing fast; is tearful and says she is having a panic attack; denies chest pain, denies any thoughts of harming herself when asked, and is able to speak in sentences between breaths
